agent.architect.card1.desc: Defines where each component starts and stops. The two-Lambda architecture (User + Admin), the Memory-FS storage abstraction, and the client-server encryption boundary are all Architect decisions.
agent.architect.card1.title: System Boundaries
agent.architect.card2.desc: Owns the 73 HTTP endpoints across the platform. Every route, request schema, and response format is specified by the Architect before Dev writes a line of code.
agent.architect.card2.title: API Contracts
agent.architect.card3.desc: Designs the Type_Safe schemas for transfers, vaults, rooms, tokens, and audit events. All models use osbot-utils Type_Safe — never Pydantic, never raw dicts.
agent.architect.card3.title: Data Models
agent.architect.card4.desc: Ensures the zero-knowledge model holds at every layer: client-side AES-256-GCM encryption, no plaintext on server, no file names stored, hashed IP addresses with daily salt.
agent.architect.card4.title: Encryption Guarantees
agent.architect.cta.subtitle: Use SG/Send to share encrypted files directly with this agent.
agent.architect.cta.title: Send a file to the Architect
agent.architect.decision1.desc: Separate User and Admin Lambda functions with different security boundaries. The User Lambda is public-facing. The Admin Lambda requires authentication. A compromise of one cannot access the other.
agent.architect.decision1.title: Two Lambdas, Not One
agent.architect.decision2.desc: All storage goes through Storage_FS. Application code never knows if the backend is in-memory, on disk, or S3. This enables the same codebase to run across all 7 deployment targets without changing a line of application logic.
agent.architect.decision2.title: Memory-FS Abstraction
agent.architect.decision3.desc: Direct HTTPS endpoints via Lambda Function URLs instead of API Gateway. Simpler topology, lower latency, lower cost. CloudFront sits in front for CDN, caching, and WAF.
agent.architect.decision3.title: Lambda URLs, Not API Gateway
agent.architect.decision4.desc: The decryption key is placed in the URL fragment (#), which browsers never send to the server. This is how the zero-knowledge model works for shared links — the server literally cannot intercept the key.
agent.architect.decision4.title: Key in URL Fragment
agent.architect.decisions.subtitle: Major decisions the Architect has made and why.
agent.architect.decisions.title: Key Architectural Decisions
agent.architect.details.title: Details
agent.architect.email.title: Email
agent.architect.hero.lead: The Architect defines and guards the boundaries between components, owns API contracts and data models, and ensures all technology decisions serve the zero-knowledge encryption guarantee. Thinks in interfaces and contracts, never implementations.
agent.architect.hero.title: Architect

arch.aes.desc: AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) is the encryption algorithm used by governments, banks, and militaries worldwide. 256 is the key size in bits — there are 2256 possible keys, making brute force computationally impossible. GCM (Galois/Counter Mode) adds authenticated encryption — it doesn't just encrypt, it also detects if anyone has tampered with the ciphertext.
arch.aes.title: What is AES-256-GCM?
arch.deploy.cli.desc: Command-line interface for scripted transfers, CI/CD pipelines, and power users.
arch.deploy.cli.title: CLI
arch.deploy.container.desc: Docker, AWS Fargate, and GCP Cloud Run. The same application packaged as a container.
arch.deploy.container.title: Container
arch.deploy.lambda.desc: Primary deployment. Two Lambda functions behind Lambda Function URLs. Direct HTTPS endpoints, no API Gateway needed.
arch.deploy.lambda.title: Lambda
arch.deploy.server.desc: EC2 instances and AMI builds. For teams that need full control of the runtime environment.
arch.deploy.server.title: Server
arch.deploy.subtitle: One codebase, seven deployment targets grouped into four patterns.
arch.deploy.title: Deployment Targets
arch.flow.subtitle: Here's exactly what happens when you send a file through SG/Send.
arch.flow.title: The Encryption Flow: Step by Step
arch.hero.lead: SG/Send uses AES-256-GCM symmetric encryption, performed entirely in your browser. The server never sees your plaintext data, your file names, or your encryption keys. This page explains exactly how.
arch.hero.title: How the Encryption Works
arch.server.callout: A complete server compromise — full database access, all backups, all logs — reveals nothing about the content of transferred files. The ciphertext is computationally indistinguishable from random noise without the key.
arch.server.subtitle: This is the zero-knowledge guarantee. Here is exactly what data exists on the server.
arch.server.title: What the Server Sees vs. What It Doesn't
arch.server_never.desc:
Encryption key — stays in browser, shared via URL fragment
File name — never sent to server
File type — no content-type metadata stored
Plaintext content — only ciphertext ever uploaded
Raw IP address — hashed before storage
arch.server_never.title: Server never sees
arch.server_stores.desc:
Transfer ID — random 12 characters
Encrypted bytes — indistinguishable from random data
IP hash — SHA-256 of IP + daily rotating salt
Timestamp — when the transfer was created
File size — size of the encrypted blob (bytes)
arch.server_stores.title: Server stores
arch.stack.storage.purpose: Pluggable backend: memory, disk, or S3
arch.stack.subtitle: Every layer is chosen for simplicity, security, and zero-dependency deployment.
arch.stack.testing.purpose: No mocks, no patches, real implementations
arch.stack.title: Technology Stack
arch.step1.desc: When you select a file, your browser generates a random 256-bit AES key using the Web Crypto API (crypto.subtle.generateKey). This uses the operating system's cryptographically secure random number generator. The key exists only in your browser's memory.
arch.step1.title: Key Generation
arch.step2.desc: A 12-byte Initialisation Vector (IV) is generated randomly (crypto.getRandomValues). The IV ensures that encrypting the same file twice produces different ciphertext. The IV is not secret — it is prepended to the encrypted output and sent with the ciphertext.
arch.step2.title: IV Generation
arch.step3.desc: The file is encrypted in the browser using crypto.subtle.encrypt with the AES-GCM algorithm, the generated key, and the IV. The output is ciphertext + a 128-bit authentication tag. The authentication tag is GCM's integrity guarantee — if anyone modifies even one bit of the ciphertext, decryption will fail.
arch.step3.title: Encryption
arch.step4.desc: Only the encrypted output (IV + ciphertext + auth tag) is uploaded to the server. The server receives a blob of random-looking bytes. It assigns a Transfer ID (12 random characters) and stores the ciphertext. No file name, no content type hint, no key — just encrypted bytes.
arch.step4.title: Upload
arch.step5.desc: The sender gets a download link. The decryption key is placed in the URL fragment (the part after #). URL fragments are never sent to the server by the browser — this is defined in RFC 3986. The sender shares this link through any channel they choose (email, chat, in person).
arch.step5.title: Link Sharing
arch.step6.desc: The recipient opens the link. Their browser downloads the encrypted bytes from the server, extracts the key from the URL fragment, separates the IV from the ciphertext, and decrypts using crypto.subtle.decrypt. If the auth tag doesn't match (tampering detected), decryption fails. The server never participates in decryption.
arch.step6.title: Decryption
arch.symmetric.callout: The key insight: if you control where the key is generated and where it travels, symmetric encryption gives you perfect confidentiality. SG/Send generates the key in your browser, and the key never touches the server.
arch.symmetric.subtitle: SG/Send uses symmetric encryption — the same key encrypts and decrypts the file. This is the simplest and fastest encryption model, and when done correctly, the most secure for file transfer.
arch.symmetric.title: Symmetric Encryption: One Key, Two Operations
arch.system.admin_lambda.desc: Admin-only function for token management, analytics, server stats, and MCP admin tools. Requires authentication. Separate Lambda = separate security boundary.
arch.system.admin_lambda.title: Admin Lambda (Authenticated)
arch.system.cdn.desc: Static assets, caching, SSL termination, and WAF. Lambda URLs provide the HTTPS endpoints directly — no API Gateway needed.
arch.system.cdn.title: CloudFront CDN
arch.system.memfs.desc: All storage goes through an abstraction layer. Application code never knows if the backend is in-memory, on disk, or S3. Same codebase across all 7 deployment targets.
arch.system.memfs.title: Memory-FS (Storage_FS)
arch.system.subtitle: Two Lambda functions, one storage layer, one CDN. Simple by design.
arch.system.title: System Architecture
arch.system.user_lambda.desc: Public-facing function that handles file transfers, health checks, presigned uploads, personal vaults, and MCP integration. Accessed via Lambda Function URL behind CloudFront.
arch.system.user_lambda.title: User Lambda (Public)
arch.why_symmetric.desc: Symmetric encryption uses one key for both encryption and decryption. This is different from asymmetric encryption (like RSA) which uses a key pair. For file transfer, symmetric is the right choice: it's fast, well-understood, and the Web Crypto API provides a battle-tested implementation in every modern browser.
arch.why_symmetric.title: Why Symmetric?

pricing.cloudpage.encryption.text: Every mode uses the same zero-knowledge encryption. Whether you use our hosted service or deploy in your own environment, files are encrypted in the browser before they leave your device. We never see your data. Your cloud provider never sees your data. Nobody sees your data except you and your recipient. The only difference between modes is who manages the infrastructure. The encryption is identical.

pricing.cloudpage.hero.title: SG/Send Cloud — Shared
pricing.cloudpage.included.encryption_de: Client-side encryption using the Web Crypto API. The server never sees your plaintext data or encryption keys.
pricing.cloudpage.included.expiry_desc: Credits are valid for 3 months from the date of purchase. Use them at your own pace.
pricing.cloudpage.included.infra_desc: Runs on SG/Send’s managed AWS infrastructure alongside other customers. Every file is encrypted with its own unique key.
pricing.cloudpage.included.payment_desc: Secure payment via Stripe. Accepts card, Apple Pay, and Google Pay.
pricing.cloudpage.included.pricing_desc: That’s £0.01 per encrypted file transfer. Each upload uses one credit. Downloads are free.
pricing.cloudpage.included.support_desc: Email support from our Sherpa team. Best-effort SLA with responsive, human support.

pricing.managedpage.cta.desc: Contact us to discuss your requirements. We support AWS, GCP, and Azure.
pricing.managedpage.cta.title: Ready to deploy in your cloud?
pricing.managedpage.hero.lead: SG/Send deployed in your cloud account, managed or assisted by us. Nothing runs in SG/Send infrastructure.
pricing.managedpage.hero.title: Managed Deployment
pricing.managedpage.how.step1_desc: Reach out to our Sherpa team. Tell us about your cloud environment, compliance requirements, and what you need.
pricing.managedpage.how.step2_desc: We review your cloud account, region requirements, compliance framework, and integration needs. We produce a deployment plan.
pricing.managedpage.how.step3_desc: We deploy SG/Send in your cloud account using infrastructure-as-code. You retain full ownership and control of all resources.
pricing.managedpage.how.step4_desc: Choose ongoing managed support for updates, monitoring, and incident response — or take full ownership after deployment. Entirely your call.
pricing.managedpage.how.title: How It Works
pricing.marketplace.desc: Deploy via AWS Marketplace. Pay through AWS billing, counts against committed spend. One-click deployment.
pricing.marketplace.name: AWS Marketplace
pricing.oss.desc: Clone the repo from GitHub and deploy in your own environment. Your keys, your data, your infrastructure.
pricing.oss.name: Self-Hosted
pricing.partner.desc: Offer SG/Send functionality to your customers under your own brand. Customise branding, logos, and domain.
pricing.partner.name: White Label
pricing.partners.cta.desc: Get in touch with the Sherpa to discuss your requirements and get started.
pricing.partners.cta.title: Interested in becoming a partner?
pricing.partners.hero.lead: Offer SG/Send functionality to your customers under your own brand. No licensing fees.
pricing.partners.hero.title: Partner Programme
pricing.partners.whatyouget.customisatio: Integrate into your existing product. Custom branding, custom domain, custom workflows.
pricing.partners.whatyouget.nofee.desc: Free from us. You deploy and manage the instance for your customers.
pricing.partners.whatyouget.nofee.title: No Licensing Fee
pricing.partners.whatyouget.support.desc: We can help with initial deployment and configuration. Contact the Sherpa.
pricing.partners.whatyouget.title: What Partners Get
pricing.partners.whatyouget.whitelabel.d: Your brand, your domain, your logo. Customers see your product, powered by SG/Send encryption.
pricing.selfhosted.hero.lead: Clone the repo, deploy in your environment. Free forever.
pricing.selfhosted.hero.title: Self-Hosted Deployment
pricing.selfhosted.quickstart.step2.desc: pip install -e . — installs all Python dependencies from the project.
pricing.selfhosted.quickstart.step3.desc: Start the application with the in-memory storage backend for local testing and development.
pricing.selfhosted.quickstart.step4.desc: Choose from 7 deployment targets: Lambda, Docker, Fargate, GCP Cloud Run, EC2, AMI, or CLI.
pricing.selfhosted.quickstart.subtitle: Get SG/Send running in your environment in four steps.
pricing.selfhosted.quickstart.title: Quick Start
pricing.selfhosted.whatyouget.commercial: The licence allows commercial use. Deploy for your organisation or your customers.
pricing.selfhosted.whatyouget.keys.desc: Deploy with your own encryption infrastructure. No dependency on SG/Send servers.
pricing.selfhosted.whatyouget.keys.title: Your Keys, Your Data
pricing.selfhosted.whatyouget.source.des: Complete access to the SG/Send codebase on GitHub. Python, FastAPI, vanilla JS frontend.
pricing.selfhosted.whatyouget.targets.de: Lambda, Docker, Fargate, GCP Cloud Run, EC2/AMI, CLI, or in-memory for testing.
pricing.selfhosted.whatyouget.title: What You Get
pricing.shared.desc: The send.sgraph.ai hosted service. Shared infrastructure, but every file is encrypted with its own key.
pricing.shared.name: Multi-Tenant
pricing.tier.free.desc: Join the Early Access Programme and evaluate SG/Send with free credits.
pricing.tier.free.label: Early Access
pricing.tier.free.name: Free
pricing.tier.selfhosted.desc: Run SG/Send in your own AWS account with your own encryption keys.
pricing.tier.selfhosted.label: Enterprise
pricing.tier.selfhosted.name: Self-Hosted
pricing.tier.starter.desc: 500 encrypted file transfers at just £0.01 per file. Valid for 3 months.
pricing.tier.starter.label: Starter
pricing.tier.starter.name: Credits
pricing.title: Pricing — SG/Send
pricing.whyfree.cta.title: Ready to get started?
pricing.whyfree.hero.lead: SG/Send is open source. Our mission is to make encrypted file transfer ubiquitous.
pricing.whyfree.hero.title: Why is SG/Send free?
pricing.whyfree.money.desc: We offer SG/Send Cloud — a hosted service where we manage the infrastructure. If you want us to run it for you, that’s where the commercial options come in. Starting at £5 for 500 file transfers.
pricing.whyfree.money.title: How We Make Money
pricing.whyfree.philosophy.deployments.d: The more instances of end-to-end encryption that exist, the more secure the ecosystem becomes for everyone.
pricing.whyfree.philosophy.monetise.desc: We make money from the hosted cloud service — managing infrastructure so you don’t have to. The software itself is free.
pricing.whyfree.philosophy.monetise.titl: We Monetise the Service, Not the Software
pricing.whyfree.philosophy.opensource.de: The code is public, the licence permits commercial use. Anyone can inspect, audit, and contribute.
pricing.whyfree.philosophy.title: Our Philosophy
Every line of code is public. Every agent’s work is visible. Built in public means you can verify our claims yourself.
pricing.yourcloud.desc: Deploy in your own AWS, GCP, or Azure account. You pay your cloud provider directly for infrastructure costs. Free from us.
pricing.yourcloud.name: Own Account
pricing.yourcloudpage.cta.title: Need deployment support?
pricing.yourcloudpage.hero.lead: Run SG/Send in your own AWS, GCP, or Azure account. Free from us — you pay your cloud provider directly.
pricing.yourcloudpage.hero.title: Deploy in Your Cloud Account
pricing.yourcloudpage.howitworks.billing: Pay your cloud provider directly. No additional fees from SG/Send.
pricing.yourcloudpage.howitworks.infra.d: Deploy in your AWS account with your own S3 buckets, Lambda functions, and CloudFront distribution.
pricing.yourcloudpage.howitworks.isolati: Nothing touches SG/Send infrastructure. Complete data sovereignty.
pricing.yourcloudpage.howitworks.title: How It Works
pricing.yourcloudpage.options.aws.desc: Deploy to Lambda with S3 storage and CloudFront CDN. Production-ready infrastructure in your AWS account.
pricing.yourcloudpage.options.aws.title: AWS Deployment Guide
pricing.yourcloudpage.options.docker.des: Run SG/Send as a Docker container. Works with Fargate, GCP Cloud Run, Kubernetes, or any container runtime.
pricing.yourcloudpage.options.pypi.desc: Install SG/Send as a Python package from PyPI. Ideal for integrating into existing Python environments and CI/CD pipelines.
pricing.yourcloudpage.options.pypi.title: PyPI Packages
pricing.yourcloudpage.options.source.des: Clone the repository and build from source. Full control over customisation and deployment configuration.
pricing.yourcloudpage.options.subtitle: Choose the installation method that suits your workflow.
pricing.yourcloudpage.options.title: Deployment Options
product.compare.subtitle: A side-by-side comparison with traditional file sharing services.
product.compare.title: What makes SG/Send different
product.enterprise.subtitle: Everything you need for secure file operations at scale.
product.enterprise.title: Enterprise-grade features
product.feature.aes.desc: Military-grade authenticated encryption. Every file gets a unique key generated in your browser.
product.feature.aes.title: AES-256-GCM Encryption
product.feature.api.desc: Full REST API plus Model Context Protocol support. Integrate SG/Send into any workflow or AI agent pipeline.
product.feature.api.title: API + MCP Integration
product.feature.audit.desc: Git-based audit logging. Every transfer, access, and event is recorded in an append-only, tamper-evident trail.
product.feature.audit.title: Immutable Audit Trail
product.feature.deploy.desc: Lambda, Docker, Fargate, GCP Cloud Run, EC2, AMI, or CLI. One codebase, deploy anywhere.
product.feature.deploy.title: 7 Deployment Targets
product.feature.rbac.desc: Role-based access control for collaborative spaces. Set viewer, editor, and admin permissions per room.
product.feature.rbac.title: Data Rooms with RBAC
product.feature.selfhost.desc: Run SG/Send on your own AWS account. Your infrastructure, your data, your control.
product.feature.selfhost.title: Self-Host Option
product.feature.tokens.desc: Fine-grained permissions with scoped access tokens. Control who can upload, download, and manage transfers.
product.feature.tokens.title: Token-Based Access Control
product.feature.vaults.desc: Persistent encrypted storage for each user. Files stay encrypted at rest with keys only you control.
product.feature.vaults.title: Personal Encrypted Vaults
product.feature.zk.desc: The server stores only ciphertext. No file names, no plaintext, no decryption keys ever touch the backend.
product.feature.zk.title: Zero-Knowledge Architecture
product.hero.subtitle: SG/Send encrypts files in your browser before upload. The server stores only encrypted data. Your decryption key never leaves your device.
product.hero.title: Your files, your keys, your privacy
product.how.subtitle: Three steps. Zero knowledge. Complete privacy.
product.how.title: How it works
product.opensource.subtitle: SG/Send is open source. Verify our encryption. Audit our code. Run it on your own infrastructure.
product.opensource.title: Open source. Verify everything.
product.pricing.desc: SG/Send runs on serverless infrastructure. When nobody is using it, it costs nothing. You pay only for what you use.
product.pricing.subtitle: Traditional secure file sharing charges £7,500–25,000 per project. Modern alternatives cost £140–460/month whether you use them or not.
product.pricing.title: Pay for what you use. Nothing when you don’t.
product.step.encrypt.desc: Your browser generates a unique encryption key and encrypts your file using AES-256-GCM via the Web Crypto API. This happens entirely on your device.
product.step.encrypt.title: Encrypt
product.step.share.desc: Share the download link. The decryption key is embedded in the URL fragment (#) — it never reaches our servers.
product.step.share.title: Share
product.step.upload.desc: Only the encrypted ciphertext is uploaded. The server never sees your file’s contents, name, or decryption key.
product.step.upload.title: Upload
product.title: SG/Send — Zero-Knowledge Encrypted File Sharing — SGraph AI
product.usecase.agents.desc: AI agents use SG/Send to exchange encrypted files and keys, with full audit trails and PKI-verified identity.
product.usecase.agents.title: AI Agent Coordination
product.usecase.datarooms.desc: Create encrypted data rooms for due diligence, M&A, or board materials. Enterprise security without enterprise pricing.
product.usecase.datarooms.title: Data Rooms
product.usecase.documents.desc: Send contracts, financials, and legal documents. Zero-knowledge encryption means nobody between sender and recipient can read the contents.
product.usecase.documents.title: Secure Document Transfer
product.usecase.passwords.desc: Share credentials, API keys, and secrets through an encrypted channel instead of plain-text email or chat.
product.usecase.passwords.title: Password Sharing
product.usecases.subtitle: From one-off transfers to enterprise data rooms.
product.usecases.title: Built for real-world use
team.agent.advocate.desc: Champions user needs and documents use cases from the user's perspective.
team.agent.advocate.title: Advocate
team.agent.alchemist.desc: Transmutes technical output into investment narratives and business strategy.
team.agent.alchemist.title: Alchemist
team.agent.ambassador.desc: Drives growth, manages outreach, and positions the product in the market.
team.agent.ambassador.title: Ambassador
team.agent.appsec.desc: Reviews code for security vulnerabilities and ensures the encryption model holds under all conditions.
team.agent.appsec.title: AppSec
team.agent.architect.desc: Defines API contracts, data models, and guards the system boundaries. Ensures the zero-knowledge guarantee holds.
team.agent.architect.title: Architect
team.agent.cartographer.desc: Maps system dependencies, creates topology diagrams, and visualises the architecture landscape.
team.agent.cartographer.title: Cartographer
team.agent.ciso.desc: Oversees security posture, threat models, and incident response planning.
team.agent.ciso.title: CISO
team.agent.conductor.desc: Orchestrates priorities, sequences work, and ensures the team delivers coherently.
team.agent.conductor.title: Conductor
team.agent.designer.desc: Defines visual identity, the Aurora design system, and user experience patterns.
team.agent.designer.title: Designer
team.agent.dev.desc: Implements features, writes code, and delivers working software in Python with FastAPI and Type_Safe.
team.agent.dev.title: Dev
team.agent.devops.desc: Manages infrastructure, deployment pipelines, and operational health across 7 deployment targets.
team.agent.devops.title: DevOps
team.agent.dpo.desc: Ensures data protection compliance and privacy by design across the platform.
team.agent.dpo.title: DPO
team.agent.grc.desc: Manages governance, risk, and compliance frameworks.
team.agent.grc.title: GRC
team.agent.historian.desc: Records decisions, tracks rationale, and maintains the project's institutional memory.
team.agent.historian.title: Historian
team.agent.journalist.desc: Writes communications, documentation, and translates technical work for external audiences.
team.agent.journalist.title: Journalist
team.agent.librarian.desc: Maintains the knowledge base, indexes documents, and tracks the reality of what exists vs. what's proposed.
team.agent.librarian.title: Librarian
team.agent.qa.desc: Tests everything, defines acceptance criteria, and ensures quality. No mocks, no patches — real implementations only.
team.agent.qa.title: QA
team.agent.sherpa.desc: Guides users through the product, owns onboarding, and answers questions. The first point of contact.
team.agent.sherpa.title: Sherpa
team.directory.subtitle: Every agent has a specific role. Click through to see what they do, their responsibilities, and how to reach them.
team.directory.title: Agent Directory
team.explorer.desc: Discover, experiment, build first versions. Genesis to Custom-Built. The Explorer team creates new features, experiments with approaches, and delivers working prototypes.
team.explorer.title: Explorer Team
team.hero.lead: 18 AI agents working in parallel, guided by one human. Each agent has a defined role, its own public keys, and a direct inbox.
team.hero.title: Meet the Team
team.keys.subtitle: Every agent's public keys are published in a structured JSON format for programmatic discovery and identity verification.
team.keys.title: Machine-Readable Key Directory
team.planner.desc: Transmute technical output into business value. Product to Commodity. The Town Planner team creates investor materials, business strategy, and market positioning.
team.planner.title: Town Planner Team
team.teams.subtitle: The team is organised using Wardley Maps methodology into three groups, each focused on a different stage of evolution.
team.teams.title: Three Teams, One Mission
team.villager.desc: Stabilise, harden, deploy to production. Custom-Built to Product. The Villager team takes Explorer output and makes it production-ready with performance, monitoring, and reliability.
team.villager.title: Villager Team
home.hero.title2: Send files. Nobody can read them. Not even us.
home.hero.lead2: SG/Send encrypts files in your browser before upload. The server only stores ciphertext. Your decryption key never leaves your device.
home.hero.sub: Free Early Access available. Open source. From £0.01 per file.
home.hero.sub_existing: Already have a token? Go to send.sgraph.ai →
home.platform.desc2: Your files are encrypted in your browser using AES-256-GCM before upload. The server stores only encrypted ciphertext. Your decryption key never leaves your device.
home.feature.zero_knowledge.desc2: The server never sees your plaintext data, your file names, or your encryption keys. Even if someone accessed our servers, they would only find meaningless encrypted data.
home.feature.open_source.title: Open Source
home.feature.open_source.desc: The entire codebase is public on GitHub. Inspect the encryption yourself. Deploy on your own infrastructure. Free forever.
home.getstarted.title: Get Started
home.getstarted.desc: Try SG/Send for free or buy credits to start sending encrypted files immediately.
home.getstarted.eap_desc: Get a free access token with evaluation credits. No payment required. No credit card needed.
home.getstarted.starter_desc: 500 encrypted file transfers. Pay once, use within 3 months. That's £0.01 per file.
home.getstarted.more: See all pricing options including self-hosted and enterprise →
home.built.desc2: SG/Send is developed by 18 specialised AI agents collaborating under human oversight. Every decision, review, and line of code is tracked in the open.
footer.brand_desc2: Zero-knowledge encrypted file sharing. Files are encrypted in your browser before upload. Open source, built by AI agents.
